name: USS PGM-9 (PGM-9)
events: 21 Oct 1944 Around 1030 K/21, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Pintado (T/Cdr. B.A. Clarey, USN) and USS Atule (T/Cdr. J.H. Maurer, USN) arrived at Saipan from Pearl Harbor. They were escorted in by USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR) which had joined around 0445 K/21.
All three submarines were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN).

22 Oct 1944 Around 0915 I/22, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN), USS Pintado (T/Cdr. B.A. Clarey, USN) and USS Atule (T/Cdr. J.H. Maurer, USN) departed Saipan for their patrol area. They were escorted out by USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR) until 1530 I/22.

24 Oct 1944 Around 0600 I/24, in position 14°40'N, 144°40'E, USS Sailfish (T/Cdr. R.E.M. Ward, USN), USS Barbel (T/Cdr. R.A. Keating, Jr., USN) and USS Parche (T/Cdr. L.P. Ramage, USN) all made rendezvous with their escort to Saipan, USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR).
The submarines arrived at Saipan around 1045 I/24. (1)

29 Oct 1944 With the repairs completed, USS Sailfish (T/Cdr. R.E.M. Ward, USN), departed Saipan around 1220 I/29 to join the other ships of her coordinated attack group in the Luzon Strait. She was escorted until 1630 I/29 by USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR). (2)
5 Nov 1944 USS Besugo (Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN) ended her 1st war patrol at Saipan. She was escorted in by USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR).
At Saipan some minor repairs were made by USS Fulton.
5 Nov 1944 Around 0500 I/5, USS Burrfish (T/Cdr. W.B. Perkins, Jr., USN) made rendezvous with USS Besugo (Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN) and their escort to Saipan, USS PGM-9 (Lt. C. Morgan, USNR).
Around 1030 I/5, USS Burrfish arrived at Saipan where she was immediately taken in hand for repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (3)
